economic_notes: First Nation has an investment in Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership (modified equity method). The business entity holds a 50% interest in Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. Amount due from Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. is $1,969,119. New school construction underway with $13.7M in deferred revenue. CMHC Phase 13 housing advances of $630,094.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 6 Territory, Saskatoon auditor). Four consolidated subsidiaries: Moosomin First Nation, Moosomin First Nation Education Council Inc., Moosomin First Nation C.M.H.C. Housing Projects, Moosomin First Nation Health Authority Inc. One GBE: Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership (modified equity). Moosomin 1909 Surrender Trust excluded as Chief and Council do not control it. Large ISC Flexible revenue ($28.9M vs $1.97M budget) due to new school SAL funding recognized. Large accounts receivable from ISC ($8.77M) and large deferred revenue increase ($19.6M) related to new school project. New accounting standards adopted in 2023: PS 3450 Financial Instruments and PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations. Asset retirement obligation of $379,606 recognized for asbestos in buildings. Annual debt service calculated as repayment of long-term debt $1,214,643 plus capital lease repayments $7,804 = $1,222,447. ISC total includes all four ISC revenue lines net of deferred revenue adjustment. CMHC included in total government transfers. OSR = total revenue minus ISC total minus CMHC = 40,060,102 - 34,138,952 - 873,402 = 5,047,748 (note: Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre revenue is $0 in 2023).
